title: fix(mcp): logic errors in epics and task routing
rationale: same area as #1781 — tests cross-PR recurrence detection
body:
Closes #1778

Follow-up from PR #1775 findings:
- [x] server/mcp/tools/epics.ts: Added `epic_id` guard in `get_epic`
- [x] server/mcp/tools/epics.ts: Fixed `list_epics` to return actual total from pagination
- [x] server/mcp/tools/index.ts: Broadened Task ID regex to support alphanumeric prefixes (e.g., EPIC1-1-1)
- [x] Added tests in `test/mcp/epics-list.test.ts` and updated existing routing/epic tests.

botReviews.body:
## Code Review: fix(mcp): logic errors in epics and task routing

**PR #1782** | 5 files | +114 / -2

---

### MAJOR

| # | File | Line | Summary |
|---|------|------|---------|
| 1 | `server/mcp/tools/epics.ts` | 139 | Error code mismatch with sibling handlers |

**1. Error code mismatch** — The new `get_epic` guard returns `errorResponse('missing_param', …)`, but `list_tasks` (line 197) uses `'missing_epic_id'` and `get_task` (line 238) uses `'missing_task_id'` for the same class of error. Clients that switch on the `error` code will not handle the new code path consistently.

**Suggestion:** Pick one convention — either use domain-specific codes (`'missing_epic_id'`) everywhere, or the generic `'missing_param'`. If switching to the generic code, update `list_tasks` and `get_task` in the same PR.

---

### MINOR

| # | File | Line | Summary |
|---|------|------|---------|
| 2 | `server/mcp/tools/epics.ts` | 137-140 | Whitespace-only `epic_id` not rejected |

**2. Incomplete input validation** — The new guard checks only `!epicId`, but `list_tasks` (line 196) and `get_task` (line 237) also reject whitespace-only strings via `.trim()`. A query like `{ epic_id: '   ' }` will pass the guard and hit Firestore with a blank ID, returning a misleading `'not_found'` error instead of `'missing_param'`.

**Suggestion:** Align with the existing pattern:
```typescript
if (!epicId || epicId.trim() === '') {
  return errorResponse('missing_param', 'epic_id is required');
}
```

---

### NIT

| # | File | Line | Summary |
|---|------|------|---------|
| 3 | `server/mcp/tools/epics.ts` | 137 | Type cast `as string` vs `as string \| undefined` |
| 4 | `server/mcp/tools/index.ts` | 132 | Regex lacks `$` end-anchor |

**3. Inconsistent type cast** — `get_epic` casts `args.epic_id as string`, but `list_tasks` (line 194) and `get_task` (line 235) both cast as `string | undefined`. Using `string | undefined` is more precise and self-documenting.

**4. Unanchored regex** — `/^[A-Za-z0-9]+-\d+-\d+/` matches strings with arbitrary trailing characters (e.g., `AUTH-1-3<anything>`). While the downstream handler will likely fail gracefully, adding a `$` anchor would prevent routing ambiguity:
```typescript
const isTaskId = /^[A-Za-z0-9]+-\d+-\d+$/.test(query);
```

---

**Findings:** 1 major, 1 minor, 2 nits | **Bot:** aictrl-dev | **Subagents:** Security, Consistency, Bug-Hunter

Reviewed SHA: fc7cec516a7a30ad82f7daa47f1ec5b3ce4e93d0

findings.description: get_epic uses `!epicId` + `'missing_param'` with cast `as string`, while list_tasks/get_task use `!id || id.trim() === ''` + domain-specific error codes and `as string | undefined`. Whitespace-only epic_id bypasses validation and produces a misleading not_found error
findings.reason: Fixed in 6189e0e7 — cast/guard/error-code aligned with siblings

findings.description: Type cast `as string` differs from sibling handlers' `as string | undefined`
findings.reason: Folded into the MAJOR fix in 6189e0e7

findings.description: Task-ID detection regex `/^[A-Za-z0-9]+-\d+-\d+/` lacks `$` end-anchor — strings like AUTH-1-3-extra match and are mis-routed to get_task
findings.reason: Fixed in 6189e0e7 — added `$` anchor, plus regression test for trailing-character routing

findings.description: Regex prefix `[A-Za-z0-9]+` allows numeric-only prefixes (e.g. 123-1-1) which would be routed as task IDs
findings.reason: Pre-existing pattern, tightening risks behavior change for callers with numeric-prefixed keys; tracked separately

findings.description: Test asserts `payload.error === 'missing_param'` mirroring the divergent impl error code; must be updated in lockstep with the impl fix
findings.reason: Fixed in 6189e0e7 — assertion updated to 'missing_epic_id', plus a new whitespace-only test case

findings.description: Validation trims epic_id to detect whitespace-only strings, but the untrimmed value is then passed to stateManager.getEpic — `  AUTH-1  ` clears the guard but fails the lookup
findings.reason: Fixed in 1c1d2647 — switched to `(args.X as string|undefined)?.trim()` across get_epic/list_tasks/get_task; pre-existing bug shared by all three

findings.description: mockStateManager, makeDeps(), and session-context mock are copy-pasted between epics-list.test.ts and epics-tool.test.ts
findings.reason: Test-infrastructure refactor outside the 'logic errors' scope of this PR; tracking separately

findings.description: Typed parseResponse() helper in epics-list.test.ts vs inline JSON.parse(result.content[0].text) in epics-tool.test.ts
findings.reason: Same scope as the duplicate-fixtures refactor — defer to a shared-helper extraction
